stem: A decision-maker must choose among 4 drug treatment options. The probability of favorable patient genotype is uncertain, estimated between 40% and 53%. The payoffs (in $K) for each option are: Drug A: $220K in good patient genotype, $123K in bad patient genotype. Drug B: $277K in good patient genotype, $142K in bad patient genotype. Drug C: $288K in good patient genotype, $119K in bad patient genotype. combination therapy: $187K in good patient genotype, $31K in bad patient genotype. Which option maximizes expected value? Does the optimal choice change across the range of probability estimates? What is the expected value of the best option?

ground_truth.answer: At P(good)=0.47: best option is Drug B with EV=$205.4K. Drug B is optimal across the entire uncertainty range. EV range of best option: [196.0, 213.6].

solution_steps: Compute EV for each option: EV = P(good)×payoff_good + P(bad)×payoff_bad.
Drug B is optimal across the entire uncertainty range.
common_failure_modes: Evaluating options only at the midpoint probability without sensitivity analysis
Not identifying the breakeven probability where the optimal choice switches
Ignoring downside risk and focusing only on expected value
Not performing minimax regret analysis to find the most robust choice
